code:
proof fn phi_2_pop_preserves_node_refs_for_remaining<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_sll: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    new_sll: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,
    ret: (T, SLLIndex),
    v: T,
)
    requires
        old_sll.wf(),
        old_sll.len() > 0,
        old_sll.unique(),
        N > 2,
        new_sll.wf(),
        new_sll.len() == old_sll.len() - 1,
        new_sll@ == old_sll@.skip(1),
        ret.0 == old_sll@[0],
        ret.1 == old_sll.get_node_ref(ret.0),
        new_sll@.contains(v),
        old_sll.get_node_ref(v) == new_sll.get_node_ref(v),
    ensures
        new_sll.get_node_ref(v) == old_sll.get_node_ref(v),
{
}

proof fn phi_4_pop_single_element_result_empty<T: Copy, const N: usize>(
    old_sll: StaticLinkedList<T, N>,

)

        old_sll.len() == 1,
        old_sll.unique(),
        N > 2,

        new_sll.len() == old_sll.len() - 1,
        new_sll@ == old_sll@.skip(1),
    ensures
        new_sll@.len() == 0,
        new_sll.free_list_len == N,
        new_sll.value_list_len == 0,
{
}
reason: After popping the last element, all N nodes must be in the free list — wf() ensures free_list_len + value_list_len == N, so this is entailed but means the free list consumes all capacity with no slack
